title: Scholar-Wendao: A Framework for Distilling Scholar Perspectives into Runnable Analytical Lenses
description: An open-source framework for distilling scholar perspectives into runnable analytical lenses for humanities researchers. Input a scholar's name or research area; the framework automatically harvests multilingual works, secondary literature, biographies, and academic controversies, then produces a 'perspective skill' — a portable analytical lens you can invoke in your research workflow rather than a chatbot to converse with. Supports two scholar types: contemporary (single-author, e.g., Stiegler, Yuk Hui) and traditional (multi-reception lineages, e.g., Aristotle, Plato, Confucius). Traditional-scholar mode produces multi-perspective output showing how each interpretive lineage reads the source. Designed for humanities-paper writing workflows — not for casual chat or persona role-play. Methodology inspired by Huashu's nuwa-skill, redesigned for academic humanities use. Bilingual: documentation in English and Chinese.

creators.name: Shen, Cong
creators.affiliation: Tianyu Vision (TIANYU VISION); MA, History of Science, Tsinghua University
